languages.text: "Эти воспоминания болезненные. Рациональное осмысление мира, — раскладывание его на гипотезы, факты, выводы, — не просто моя стезя, это суть моего существования. Мои мысли мне подвластны. Но чувства — они привносят хаос в это бытие. Терзают логику, топчут расчеты. Если я избавлюсь от фигмента Нунциуса, то останусь наедине с чудовищной неподъемной задачей. Моя эффективность упадет, путь к Познанию заволочет туманом. Парадоксально, мое безумие — залог сохранности моего разума и духа".

"These memories are painful. Forming a rational understanding of the world — breaking it down into hypotheses, facts, conclusions — is not just my vocation, it is the essence of my existence. I have power over my thoughts. But my emotions — they bring chaos to my existence. They tear through logic and trample over calculations. If I rid myself of the Nuncius figment, I will be left facing a monstrous, insurmountable task alone. My efficiency will plummet, and the path to Knowledge will be shrouded in mist. Paradoxically, my insanity is the key to preserving my mind and soul."
languages.original_text: "Эти воспоминания болезненные. Рациональное осмысление мира, — раскладывание его на гипотезы, факты, выводы, — не просто моя стезя, это суть моего существования. languages.text: «Los recuerdos son dolorosos. Formular una comprensión racional del mundo, desglosarlo en hipótesis, hechos y conclusiones no es solo mi vocación, es la esencia de mi ser. Controlo mis pensamientos, pero mis emociones sumen mi existencia en el caos. Desgarran la lógica y pisotean mis cálculos, Si me librara de la ilusión de Nuncius, quedaría solo ante una tarea monumental e insuperable. Mi eficacia se desplomaría y el camino hacia el conocimiento quedaría tapado por niebla. Paradójicamente, es mi locura la que preserva mi mente y mi alma».
languages.original_text: "These memories are painful. Forming a rational understanding of the world — breaking it down into hypotheses, facts, conclusions — is not just my vocation, it is the essence of my existence. I have power over my thoughts. But my emotions — they bring chaos to my existence. They tear through logic and trample over calculations. Mi eficacia se desplomaría y el camino hacia el conocimiento quedaría tapado por niebla. Paradójicamente, es mi locura la que preserva mi mente y mi alma».

languages.text: “这些记忆太痛苦了。理性地认知世界——将其分解为假设、事实和结论——不仅是我的职业，更是我存在的本质。我能掌控自己的思想，但我的情绪……却会给我的存在带来混乱。它们撕裂逻辑，践踏计算。抛弃努恩修斯这个幻象，我就要独自面对一项无法逾越的艰巨任务。我的效率会暴跌，求知之路也会被迷雾笼罩。悖论在于，恰恰是我的疯狂，保全了我的心智和灵魂。”
languages.original_text: "These memories are painful. Forming a rational understanding of the world — breaking it down into hypotheses, facts, conclusions — is not just my vocation, it is the essence of my existence. I have power over my thoughts. But my emotions — they bring chaos to my existence. They tear through logic and trample over calculations. If I rid myself of the Nuncius figment, I will be left facing a monstrous, insurmountable task alone. My efficiency will plummet, and the path to Knowledge will be shrouded in mist. Paradoxically, my insanity is the key to preserving my mind and soul."

languages.text: « Ces souvenirs sont douloureux. J'ai passé ma vie à décomposer notre univers en hypothèses, en faits et en conclusions afin d'en développer une compréhension rationnelle. Plus qu'une simple vocation, c'est l'essence même de mon existence. Je suis capable de maîtriser mes pensées, mais mes émotions sont source de chaos. Elles anéantissent la logique et se moquent éperdument des calculs. Si je me débarrasse du Nuncius créé par mon imagination, je me retrouverai seul face à une tâche monstrueuse et insurmontable. Mon efficacité dégringolera et la voie de la connaissance sera plongée dans le brouillard. Aussi paradoxal que ce soit, ma folie est la clé qui préserve mon esprit et mon âme. »
languages.original_text: "These memories are painful. Forming a rational understanding of the world — breaking it down into hypotheses, facts, conclusions — is not just my vocation, it is the essence of my existence. I have power over my thoughts. If I rid myself of the Nuncius figment, I will be left facing a monstrous, insurmountable task alone. My efficiency will plummet, and the path to Knowledge will be shrouded in mist. Paradoxically, my insanity is the key to preserving my mind and soul."

Mon efficacité dégringolera et la voie de la connaissance sera plongée dans le brouillard. Aussi paradoxal que ce soit, ma folie est la clé qui préserve mon esprit et mon âme. »

languages.text: "Bu anılar acı verici. Dünyayı rasyonel bir şekilde anlamlandırmak... Onu hipotezlere, gerçeklere, sonuçlara ayırmak; yalnızca mesleğim değil, varoluşumun özüdür. Düşüncelerim üzerinde gücüm var. Fakat duygularım, varoluşuma kaos getiriyor. Mantığı parçalıyor ve hesaplamaları ezip geçiyorlar. Kendimi Nuncius kuruntusundan kurtarırsam korkunç, aşılmaz bir vazifeyle tek başıma yüzleşmek zorunda kalacağım. Verimliliğim düşecek ve İrfan yolu, sislere gömülecek. Paradoksal bir şekilde deliliğim, zihnimi ve ruhumu korumanın anahtarı."
languages.original_text: "These memories are painful. Forming a rational understanding of the world — breaking it down into hypotheses, facts, conclusions — is not just my vocation, it is the essence of my existence. I have power over my thoughts. But my emotions — they bring chaos to my existence. They tear through logic and trample over calculations. If I rid myself of the Nuncius figment, I will be left facing a monstrous, insurmountable task alone. My efficiency will plummet, and the path to Knowledge will be shrouded in mist. Paradoxically, my insanity is the key to preserving my mind and soul."

Düşüncelerim üzerinde gücüm var. Fakat duygularım, varoluşuma kaos getiriyor. Mantığı parçalıyor ve hesaplamaları ezip geçiyorlar. Kendimi Nuncius kuruntusundan kurtarırsam korkunç, aşılmaz bir vazifeyle tek başıma yüzleşmek zorunda kalacağım. Verimliliğim düşecek ve İrfan yolu, sislere gömülecek. Paradoksal bir şekilde deliliğim, zihnimi ve ruhumu korumanın anahtarı."

languages.text: "Diese Erinnerungen sind schmerzhaft. Die Bildung eines rationalen Verständnisses der Welt ... ihre Aufschlüsselung in Hypothesen, Fakten und Schlüsse ... das ist nicht nur meine Berufung, sondern die Essenz meiner Existenz. Ich habe Macht über meine Gedanken. Aber meine Gefühle ... sie bringen Chaos in meine Existenz. Sie strafen jede Logik Lügen und trampen auf Berechnungen herum. Befreie ich mich von dem Nuncius-Hirngespinst, stehe ich allein vor einer monströsen, unlösbaren Aufgabe. Dann wird meine Effizienz schwinden und der Weg zum Wissen im Nebel verschwinden. Paradoxerweise kann allein mein Wahnsinn meinen Verstand und meine Seele retten."
languages.original_text: "These memories are painful. Paradoxerweise kann allein mein Wahnsinn meinen Verstand und meine Seele retten."

languages.text: "これらの記憶は痛みを伴う。世界を合理的に理解し、仮説、事実、結論に落とし込むことは、私の天職であると同時に、私の存在の本質でもある。私は自分の考えを支配する力を持っている。しかし、私の感情は......私の存在に混沌をもたらす。論理を切り裂き、計算を踏みにじる。もし私がヌンシウスの形象を消し去れば、私は巨大で克服不可能な仕事にたった一人で立ち向かうことになる。私の能率は急落し、知識への道は霧に包まれるだろう。逆説的だが、私の狂気は私の精神と魂を守る鍵なのだ」。
languages.original_text: "These memories are painful. Forming a rational understanding of the world — breaking it down into hypotheses, facts, conclusions — is not just my vocation, it is the essence of my existence. I have power over my thoughts. But my emotions — they bring chaos to my existence. They tear through logic and trample over calculations. If I rid myself of the Nuncius figment, I will be left facing a monstrous, insurmountable task alone. My efficiency will plummet, and the path to Knowledge will be shrouded in mist. Paradoxically, my insanity is the key to preserving my mind and soul."
